title: Crystal gravfield trap
content:

A '''crystal gravfield trap''' ('''CGT''') was an expensive type of [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] that used a synthetic [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] grid to detect and identify fluctuations in the local [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] field. High quality crystal gravfield traps could detect and identify fluctuations for hundreds of thousands of [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] around the device. They could be blocked by the presence of mass, not being able to detect a [[Starship/Legends|ship]] on the far side of a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] due to its mass.<ref name="SWS" />

CGTs were the only sensor that could reliably detect a ship with an active [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]]. [[Bartokk]] assassins hired by [[Groodo]] the Hutt had access to a CGT sensor and thus were able to detect the cloaked [[Scimitar/Legends|Sith Infiltrator]] of [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]].

When [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] blockaded [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with a number of cloaked [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]], locating them became a top priority. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] did not possess any CGTs, and so was required to attempt to [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|steal]] one from the Imperial base at [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi]]. New Republic [[General]] [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] had one before the [[Siege of Svivren]], but it became lost.<ref name="The Last Command">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref>

The [[LightStealth-18 Reconnaissance Ship]], used by the New Republic during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], was equipped with such devices as part of its sensor array.

The [[Imperial Remnant]] had [[Imperial Patrol Frigate]]s equipped with crystal gravfield traps and used as deep-space probe ships.
